title: An apparatus operable as a set-top unit
abstract: One aspect of the invention provides an apparatus operable as a set-top unit and having an operating system which permits two-way or bidirectional packet-based data communication services and a digital 5 television system which permits digital broadcast services. Applications may be enabled to be executed on a modified application framework associated with the operating system to perform media processing operations and generate media contents based on performed media processing operations. Also included in the apparatus is a media interaction manager that can be 10 executed on the application framework to generate media interaction data based on interactions of a user with the media contents via a user access interface which operably supports controlled communications between any one of the applications and the application framework, between the application framework and the operating system, and between the operating 15 system and the digital television system.
claims:
Claims An apparatus operable as a set-top unit capable of supporting packet-based data communication and digital broadcast services, the apparatus comprising: a memory system; a plurality of hardware devices, each of the plurality of hardware devices being coupled to the memory system; a plurality of applications stored in the memory system, designed based on an application framework, and executable on the application framework from the memory system to perform media processing operations and generate media contents based on one or more performed media processing operations utilizing the plurality of hardware devices; an operating system permitting the packet-based data communication services, enabling execution of the application framework, and including a plurality of libraries and a kernel having a binder driver and drivers matching and supporting the plurality of hardware devices; a digital television system permitting the digital broadcast services and integrated into the operating system such that the digital television system is in operative communication with at least one of the plurality of applications when the binding driver is utilized by any one of the plurality of applications to access the digital broadcast services; and 

 a user access interface operably supporting controlled communications between the any one of the plurality of applications and the application framework, between the application framework and the operating system, and between the operating system and the digital television system, wherein a media interaction manager is stored on the memory system and executable on the application framework from the memory system to generate media interaction data based on interactions of a user with the media contents via the user access interface. The apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the media interaction manager is further executed to analyze the media interaction data. The apparatus according to claim 2, wherein the media interaction data analyzed by the media interaction manager are arranged to be stored, by at least one of the plurality of hardware devices, in the memory system. The apparatus according to claim 3, wherein the media interaction data analyzed by the media interaction manager are arranged to be transmitted, by the at least one of the plurality of hardware devices, to an external electronic device over a packet-based communication network. The apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the executed media interaction manager causes rendering on the user access interface of graphical representations of a plurality of mood media items. The apparatus according to claim 5, wherein the executed media interaction manager can be caused to receive at least one mood 

 information indicative of any one of the plurality of mood media items based on a selection input by the user of the any one of the plurality of mood media items identifying the at least one mood information through the user access interface. The apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the digital television system supports any of an electronic program guide service and a video-on-demand service. The apparatus according to claim 7, wherein the electronic program guide service provides on the user access interface television schedule information organized according to at least time information and channel information. The apparatus according to claim 7, wherein the video-on-demand service provides a television program viewable on the user access interface and selectable from a set of television programs in accordance with the digital broadcast services. The apparatus according to claim 1, wherein one or more advertisement information can be served on the user access interface based on the media interaction data analyzed by the media interaction manager. The apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the media interaction data may include one or more metrics. The apparatus according to claim 11, wherein one or more advertisement information can be served on the user access interface based on the one or more metrics. 

 The apparatus according to claim 11, wherein the one or more metrics include information representative of a number of times a television program is viewed on the user access interface by the user within any given time period. The apparatus according to claim 11, wherein the one or more metrics include information representative of a length of time that a television program is viewed on the user access interface by the user within any given time period. The apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the plurality of applications include at least one application from an application market based on a selection by the user. The apparatus according to claim 15, wherein the application market is any one of Android's Google Play, Apple's App Store, Microsoft's Windows Phone Store, Amazon's AppStore, AppsLib, SlideME, and BlackBerry's App World. The apparatus according to claim 11, wherein the one or more metrics include information representative of a number of times at least one application is downloaded from an application market by a plurality of users. The apparatus according to claim 11, wherein the one or more metrics include information indicative of a number of times a string of characters occurs on the any one of the plurality of applications based on inputs of the user. 19. The apparatus according to claim 11, wherein the one or more metrics include information indicative of a number of times at least one mood information is received by the executed media interaction 

 manager based on selection inputs by a plurality of users for any one of a set of television programs viewed on the user access interface by the plurality of users. The apparatus according to claim 11, wherein the one or more metrics include information indicative of a ratio of a total number of times that at least one mood information is received by the executed media interaction manager to a total number of times that each mood information of a plurality of mood media items other than the one indicating the at least one mood information is received by the executed media interaction manager.
